selection_reasons.repair-falsify-5x3-efficient: pooled champion across 8 fresh panels: 45/102 exact; 41.2% weakest family
selection_reasons.repair-falsify-9x3: current panel winner retained unchanged for replication

source: mechanical anchors plus iteration 11 Sol director

strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: A five-answer bank paired with the validated three-reviewer falsification mechanism may retain much of the champion's accuracy at eight calls per case rather than twelve; earlier five-solver repair systems reached 0.417–0.500 pooled accuracy.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Expanding the shared independent bank from five to nine may improve exact-answer coverage and stabilize candidate support, while retaining the only review mechanism with a positive observed correction balance.
strategies.name: Nine Solvers plus Three Falsifying Repairers

strategies.hypothesis: An unchanged third-panel replication tests whether the cumulative 10-to-2 helpful-to-harmful intervention balance is real and whether Iteration 11 was a noisy reversal. Paired with five-by-three on the same bank, it directly tests reviewer dilution at four fewer calls per case.
strategies.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Sequential falsification may prevent three reviewers from fragmenting into unrelated repairs: each later reviewer must attack the immediately preceding proposal before revising it. Its 7/18 checkpoint result warrants replication as an orthogonal organization.
strategies.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination
